case for review under Order XLVII Rule 1 of the Supreme Court
Rules 2013 has been established.

being defective as well as on merits.

(vi) of paragraph 2 of the order dated 11.12.2025 are modified, and
the same shall now read as follows:

i.e., on every second Saturday, to the jurisdictional
Police Station.

--- Page 878 ---
2(vi). The police of the States of Rajasthan and Madhya
Pradesh may monitor the day-to-day activities of the
petitioner and proceed against him in accordance with law
if he is found involved in any other offence.

vide order dated 11.12.2025 is made absolute.
3. The Special Leave Petition is, accordingly, disposed of.
4. All pending applications, if any, also stand disposed of.

now, till 08.09.2026. This shall be subject to final outcome of the

22.07.2026.
3. Application (IA No. 204733/2026) is, accordingly, disposed of.

ITEM NO.9               COURT NO.1               SECTION IX
               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Petition(s) for Special Leave to Appeal (C) No(s).23743/2026
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated 06-03-2026
in PIL No.63/2024 passed by the High Court of Judicature at Bombay]
PRASHANT JAYANT RAUL & ANR.                        Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
THE PIMPRI-CHINCHWAD MUNICIPAL CORPORATION & ORS.  Respondent(s)
IA No. 198247/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T., IA No. 199227/2026
- PERMISSION TO FILE ADDITIONAL DOCUMENTS/FACTS/ANNEXURES
 
Date : 21-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE THE CHIEF JUSTICE
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE JOYMALYA BAGCHI
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE VIPUL M. PANCHOLI
For Petitioner(s) :Ms. Uttara Babbar, Sr. Adv.
                   Mr. N. Sai Vinod, AOR
                   Ms. Kanu Garg, Adv.
                   Mr. Savyasachi Narayanan, Adv.
                                      
For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. The controversy pertains to a parcel of land to which the
petitioners seek their access on the assumption that it may be
treated as a public park. The record reveals that the land was
given  to  Maharashtra  Metro  Corporation  for  compulsory
afforestation. It is also an admitted fact that subsequently the
land has been handed over to the Election Commission of India (ECI)
for the storage of  Voter Verifiable Paper Audit Trail  (VVPAT)
Godown and Regional Training Centre and also for storage of
Electronic Voter Machine Ballot Boxes. 
1

--- Page 882 ---
2. In our considered opinion, no mandamus can be issued to get
the plot vacated from ECI and to then declare it as a public park,
to provide access to the petitioners. The appropriate recourse for
the petitioners would be to approach the ECI and seek limited
access to the subject land for recreational purposes without
compromising the security and safety of the godown. 
3. We have no reason to doubt that the ECI will consider the
representation sympathetically in consultation with the Municipal
Authorities.
4. The Special Leave Petition is, accordingly, disposed of.
5. All pending applications, if any, also stand disposed of.
(ARJUN BISHT)                               (PREETHI DILEEP KUMAR)
ASTT. REGISTRAR-cum-PS                        DY. REGISTRAR
2

Petition(s) for Special Leave to Appeal (Crl.) No(s). 12893/2026
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated 15-07-2026
in CRLMB No. 1307/2026 passed by the High Court of Delhi at New
Delhi]
GULSHAN PAHUJA                                     Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
COURTS ON ITS OWN MOTION HONBLE DELHI 
HIGH COURT & ANR.   Respondent(s)
IA No. 204481/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING C/C OF THE IMPUGNED 
JUDGMENT
IA No. 204482/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T.
 
Date : 20-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE DIPANKAR DATTA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE SHEEL NAGU
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. Manish Jain, Adv.
                   Mr. Vikash Kumar Verma, Adv.
                   Mr. Shanky Jain, Adv.
                   Mr. Jugul Kishor Gupta, AOR
                   Mr. Vasim Akhter, Adv.
                   Mr. Vinay Jain, Adv.
                                      
For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. The Delhi High Court by the impugned order dated 15 th July, 2026 has
rejected the petitioner’s prayer for extending the time for him to surrender. 
2. Incidentally, the petitioner was found guilty of contempt vide judgment of
conviction dated 21st April, 2026 and sentenced to 6 (six) months imprisonment
together with fine vide order dated 16th May, 2026. The sentence was, however,
suspended till 15th July, 2026.
3. It is not in dispute that the petitioner has filed an appeal under Section 19

--- Page 894 ---
2
of the Contempt of Courts Act, 1971 challenging the judgment of conviction and
the order on sentence, vide Diary No. 37740/2026; however, the appeal could
not be placed for consideration before the appropriate Bench because the same
is defective.
4. In the meantime, before this special leave petition could be taken up for
consideration, the petitioner has reportedly surrendered.
5. We  have  ascertained  from  the  learned  counsel  appearing  for  the
petitioner  that several documents annexed to the appeal are  in vernacular
language and have to be translated and it has not been possible for him to cure
the defects. It is also submitted that since the petitioner is now in custody, he
cannot cure the defects without being released.
6. We grant liberty to the petitioner to file an application for exemption from
filing official translation.
7. Upon filing of such application, the Registry shall place the appeal under
Section  19  of  the  Contempt  of  Courts  Act  before  this  Bench  or  any  other
appropriate  Bench,  subject  to  obtaining  orders  from  the  Hon’ble  the  Chief
Justice of India.
8. Since this special leave petition has become infructuous, the same is
disposed of.
(JATINDER KAUR)                             (SUDHIR KUMAR SHARMA)
P.S. to REGISTRAR                             COURT MASTER (NSH)

Criminal Appeal  No(s).  3030/2026
KARNI SINGH                                        Appellant(s)
                                VERSUS
STATE OF RAJASTHAN                                 Respondent(s)
[ONLY CONSIDERATION OF BAIL IS LISTED UNDER THIS ITEM.] 
 
Date : 21-07-2026 This appeal was called on for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE K.V. VISWANATHAN
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE ARUN PALLI
For Appellant(s) : 
                   Mr. Ajit Sharma, AOR
                   Mr. Kanchan Kumar, Adv.
                   Mr. Yuvrajsinh C Solanki, Adv.
                   Mr. Anant Ram Mishra, Adv.
                   Mr. Lareb Habib Ansari, Adv.
                   Ms. Shweta Jain, Adv.
                   Mr. Ashutosh Senger, Adv.
                   Mr. Shaswat Koushik, Adv.                   
                   
For Respondent(s) : 
                   Mr. Padmesh Mishra, A.A.G.
                   Mr. S. Udaya Kumar Sagar, AOR
                                     
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Heard Mr. Ajit Sharma, learned counsel appearing
for the appellant and Mr. Padmesh Mishra, learned counsel
appearing  for  the  respondent-State  on  suspension  of
sentence and grant of bail.
Criminal Appeal No.3030/2026 Page 1 of 2

--- Page 910 ---
2. Learned counsel appearing for the appellant submits
that the incident is of 1988.  The appellant was on bail
throughout the pendency of the case before the High
Court.  Learned counsel also submits that the appellant
was not named in the First Information Report and further
that in the identification memo for the TI parade the
appellant had told the Magistrate that witnesses had seen
the appellant before the TI parade. In any event, these
are the matters to be decided at the time of hearing the
appeal.
3. Mr.  Padmesh  Mishra,  learned  counsel  for  the
respondent-State submits that the appellant ought not to
be enlarged on bail, since he is convicted for an offence
under Section 302 of the Indian Penal Code, 1860
4. Having  considered  the  overall  facts  and
circumstances, we are of the opinion that since hearing
of the appeal will take some time and there are various
aspects to be considered in the appeal, the sentence of
the appellant shall stand suspended.
5. We direct that pending appeal, the sentence of the
appellant is suspended and the appellant is directed to
be enlarged on bail subject to the satisfaction of the
Trial Court.
6. The prayer for bail is accordingly disposed of.
(ANITA MALHOTRA)                          (MANOJ KUMAR)
     DEPUTY REGISTRAR                          COURT MASTER
Criminal Appeal No.3030/2026 Page 2 of 2

such terms and conditions as may be imposed by the Trial
Court, subject to the condition that the petitioner shall
continue to co-operate with the trial/investigation.

above terms.

disposed of.

                             O R D E R
1. Delay condoned.
2. Exemption Application is allowed.
3. We do not find any error not to speak of any error of law in
the impugned Judgment passed by the High Court dismissing the
Criminal Appeal preferred by the petitioner.
4. However,  taking  into  consideration  the  fact  that  the
petitioner has been undergoing life imprisonment past 14 years, it
shall  be  open  for  the  petitioner  to  prefer  an  appropriate
representation to the Competent Authority of the State Government
including remission of sentence.

--- Page 950 ---
2

an appropriate decision on the same in accordance with law, more
particularly,  the  remission  policy  prevalent  as  on  date  the
commission of the offence or any other policy which is more
beneficial to the petitioner – convict.

7. Pending applications, if any, also stand disposed of.

                   Ms. Sneha Jankiraman, Adv.
                   Mr. Siddhant Grover, Adv.
                   Ms. Roshni Ojha, Adv.
                   M/s Khaitan & Co., AOR
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. We  have  heard  Mr.  Shyam  Mehta,  learned
senior counsel for the petitioners and Dr. A. M.
Singhvi, learned senior counsel for the respondent.
2. In the facts and circumstances of the case,
we are of the view that the impugned judgment has

--- Page 996 ---
2 SLP(C)  No.  6129/2026
recorded available findings and hence, we see no
ground to entertain the Special Leave Petition.
3. Accordingly,  the  Special  Leave  Petition
stands dismissed.
4. Learned senior counsel appearing for the
petitioners argues that the view of the High Court
in paragraph nos. ‘66’  and ‘67’ of the impugned
judgment are either harsh or avoidable, for the
steps taken by the petitioners before us are in
bona fide belief of its jurisdiction or discretion.
He requests the Court to make the cost easy and
expunge the costs imposed by the impugned judgment.
5. We  have  perused  the  reasoning  in  the
impugned judgment. We are of the view that the
prayer  made  by  the  petitioners  is  reasonable.
Consequently, the direction for the imposition of
cost of Rs.25,00,000/-(Rupees twenty-five lakhs) in
the impugned judgment is interfered with and set
aside by us.
6. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand
disposed of.
(SNEHA DAS)                                 (NIDHI MATHUR)
   COURT MASTER (SH)                           COURT MASTER (NSH)

                             O R D E R

the petitioners.
2. We see no reason to interfere with the
impugned order passed by the High Court.
3. The Special Leave Petition is, accordingly,
dismissed.
4. After  the  order  is  dictated,  learned

--- Page 1004 ---
2 SLP(C)  No.  23976/2026
counsel, Mr. Swarup, requests the Court to grant
one year’s time to the petitioners to hand over
vacant possession of the subject-premises to the
respondent-landlord.
5. We are informed that the petitioners have
been tenants of the respondent(s) for over four
decades.
6. Taking note of the request made by the
learned  counsel  for  the  petitioners,  we  are
inclined to grant six months’ time from today to
the petitioners to hand over vacant and peaceful
possession  to  the  respondent-landlord.  To  that
effect, the petitioner shall file an undertaking on
affidavit in this Court within two weeks from today
and will continue to pay/deposit the agreed rent
between the parties till they vacate the subject-
premises.
7. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand
disposed of.

Yesterday, this Court passed the following 
order:-
“Mr. Amit Anand Tiwari, learned senior counsel
appearing  for  the  respondent(s),  upon
instructions, states that the respondents herein,
who are the original writ petitioners before the
SLP (C) No.   17415/2026  1

--- Page 1040 ---
High Court, wish to withdraw the writ petition.
However, they are unable to do so as there is an
interim order operating from this Court. 
List the special leave petition along with
application(s) tomorrow, i.e. 21.07.2026.”
Today, Mr. Mukul Rohtagi and Mr. Amit Anand
Tiwari, learned senior counsels appearing for respondent
no. 1 reiterate the above prayer and state that writ
petition bearing no. 19287 of 2026 pending before the
High Court may be allowed to be withdrawn. 
In that view of the matter, I.A. No.  166544 of
2026 stands allowed. 
The Writ Petition bearing no. 19287 of 2026
pending before the High Court stands withdrawn. Formal
orders may be passed by the High Court upon receipt of
copy of this order.
The interim stay granted by this Court  vide
order dated 13.05.2026 stands discharged. 
The Special Leave Petition stands disposed of.
The pending application(s), if any, shall stand
disposed of.

          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
Learned ASG, on instructions, submits that in
view of the subsequent developments whereby, the
application filed by the respondent to quash the

--- Page 1114 ---
2
criminal proceedings arising out of the predicate
FIRs has been dismissed by the High Court in favour
of  the  predicate  investigating  agency  and,
therefore,  nothing  further  survives  for
consideration in the present Special Leave Petition
as  the  petitioner-ED  can  now  proceed  with  the
investigation under the PMLA, 2002.
Accordingly, the Special Leave Petition stands
dismissed.
However, we make it clear that the observations
and the reasoning given by the High Court in the
impugned order while staying the investigation under
the PMLA, 2002 shall stand set aside and will have
no bearing on the subsequent proceedings.
Pending  application(s),  if  any,  shall  stand
disposed of.
DIARY NO.41917/2026
Taken on Board.
List the matter next week.

IA No. 194363/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING C/C OF THE IMPUGNED

IA No. 194364/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T.
 
Date : 20-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE M.M. SUNDRESH
         HON'BLE  MR. JUSTICE PRASANNA B. VARALE
For Petitioner(s) Mr. Jitendra Sethi, Sr. Adv.
                   Mr. Keshav Sethi, Adv.
                   Mr. Bharat Kashyap, Adv.
                   Ms. Aanchal, Adv.
                   Mr. Divyam Gaur, Adv.
                   Mr. Shobhit Dimri, Adv.
                   Mr. Anmol Anand, AOR
                   
For Respondent(s) Mr. Suryaprakash V. Raju, A.S.G.
                   Mr. Shreekant Neelappa Terdal, AOR
                   Mr. Zoheb Hossain, Adv.
                   Mr. Annam Venkatesh, Adv.
                   Mr. Bhuvan Kapoor, Adv.
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
We  have  heard  the  learned  Senior  counsel
appearing for the petitioner and the learned ASG
appearing for the respondent-CBI.

--- Page 1116 ---
2
Learned  Senior  counsel  appearing  for  the
petitioner placed reliance upon the decision of this
Court in Vihaan Kumar v. State of Haryana & Anr . 2025
INSC 162 which, in our considered view, cannot be the
basis for entertaining this Special Leave Petition as
the position on the issue raised was clarified by a
subsequent decision of a Coordinate Bench of this
Court in Mihir Rajesh Shah v. State of Maharashtra
and Anr. - 2025 INSC 1288.
Hence, we find no ground to interfere with the
impugned order passed by the High Court.
The  Special  Leave  Petition  is,  accordingly,
dismissed.
Pending  application(s),  if  any,  shall  stand
disposed of.
(ASHA SUNDRIYAL)                                (POONAM VAID)
DEPUTY REGISTRAR                             ASSISTANT REGISTRAR

For Petitioner(s) :Mr. B. Karunakaran, AOR
                   Ms. Jayasri, Adv.
                                      
For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel, the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
Delay condoned.
Learned Senior counsel appearing for the petitioner
submits that the scope and applicability of Rule 11(3) and
Rule 7(1) of the Tamil Nadu Pension Rules, 1978 has not
been considered by the High Court in the impugned judgment.
1

--- Page 1134 ---
Taking note of the said submission, we grant liberty to
the petitioner to file a review petition before the High
Court, within a period of four weeks from the date of
receipt of a copy of this order, in which case the
question of limitation shall not arise.
Liberty  is  also  granted  to  the  petitioner  to
challenge the impugned order before us, in the event of
the review petition being dismissed.
Till the disposal of the said review petition, the
High Court shall not proceed with the Contempt proceedings
initiated in Contempt Petition (MD) No.1636/2026.
The  Special  Leave  Petition  is  disposed  of,
accordingly. 
Pending application(s), if any, shall also stand
disposed of.

Petition for Special Leave to Appeal (C) No.11093/2026
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated 05-12-2025 
in CR No. 7899/2025 passed by the High Court of Punjab & Haryana at
Chandigarh]
MAHENDRA NARENDRA CHARITABLE EDUCATION 
TRUST (REGD.) & ORS. Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
SAMUNDER SINGH & ORS.                              Respondent(s)
IA No. 138912/2026 - APPLICATION FOR VACATION OF INTERIM ORDER
IA No. 95120/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T.
IA No. 145473/2026 - PERMISSION TO FILE ADDITIONAL 
DOCUMENTS/FACTS/ANNEXURES
IA No. 95115/2026 - PERMISSION TO FILE ADDITIONAL 
DOCUMENTS/FACTS/ANNEXURES
 
Date : 17-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE M.M. SUNDRESH
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE PRASANNA B. VARALE
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. Gagan Gupta, Sr. Adv.
                   Ms. Natasha Dalmia, AOR
                   Ms. Anisha Jain, Adv.
                   Ms. Shambhavi Singh, Adv.
                   Ms. Prerna Cheema, Adv.
                                      
For Respondent(s) :Mr. Siddharth Batra, AOR
                   Mr. Chinmay Dubey, Adv.
                   Mr. Akshit Lunial, Adv.
                   Ms. Shivani Chawla, Adv.
                   Mr. Mehul Gulati, Adv.
                   
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
Though arguments have been made at length by the
learned counsel appearing for the parties, inasmuch as the
issue arises out of an interim order passed under Order 39
Rule 1 and 2 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, we are
1

--- Page 1140 ---
not inclined to go into the merits of the case, except
stating that status quo as on the date of filing of the
Special Leave Petition, shall be maintained by the parties
during the pendency of the suit.
We further direct the trial Court to expedite the
hearing of the suit, and make an endeavour to complete it
within a period of six weeks from the date receipt of a
copy of this order.
We make it clear that both the parties shall not
take  any  decision  pertaining  to  the  plaintiff-trust
without the permission of the trial Court.
We further direct that for withdrawing of any amount
of more than Rs.10,00,000/-, permission of the Trial Court
shall be obtained.
We also make it clear that cash salary will have to
be paid from the school account only.
With  the  above  directions,  the  Special  Leave
Petition stands disposed of.
Pending application(s), if any, shall also stand
disposed of.
(KRITIKA TIWARI)                                  (POONAM VAID)
COURT MASTER (SH)                             ASSISTANT REGISTRAR
2

IN THE SUPREME COURT OF INDIA
CIVIL APPELLATE JURISDICTION
CIVIL APPEAL  NO(S).  5330/2013
V.K. JOHN                                          Appellant(s)
                                VERSUS
MANSOOR ALI KHAN                                   Respondent(s)
WITH
C.A. No. 5329/2013
ORDER
Heard the learned counsel appearing for the appellant
and the respondent(s).
We have also perused the impugned judgment. Suffice it
to state that the impugned judgment does not warrant any
interference as right of the appellant to take further
appropriate steps is not foreclosed.
We have also been informed that after the impugned
order was passed, a final decree has also been issued.
In such view of the matter, liberty is given to the
appellant to work out the remedy in the manner known to
law. All issues are left open.
The appeals are dismissed, accordingly.   
Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed
of.

         UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. The learned counsel for the petitioner submits
that after the interim protection granted by this
Court, the prosecution had sought to arrest the
petitioner in different cases. In some of those
cases on 23.04.2026, interim protection was granted
by the High Court and that matter is pending. There
is another case relating to Enforcement Directorate
in which anticipatory bail has been applied for and
the matter is likely to be listed before the High
Court tomorrow. In such circumstances, request on
behalf of the petitioner is to adjourn this matter
for a period of two weeks.
2. Having regard to the above, without expressing
any opinion on the merits of the other cases, let
this matter be listed on 07.08.2026.
3. In the meantime, we deem it appropriate to
require the petitioner to surrender his passport
before the Registrar of this Court within 48 hours.
4. Subject to above, the interim order granted
earlier to continue.

For Petitioner(s)  Mr. Shovan  Mishra, AOR
                    
For Respondent(s)  Miss Vidushi, AOR
                   Mr. Jai Dehadrai, Adv.
                   
                   
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Refiling delay condoned.
2. This Special Leave Petition is reported to be
beyond time by 216 days. There is no satisfactory
explanation for the delay. 
3. Besides, by the impugned order, the High Court
dismissed the State’s appeal on ground of delay of
1

--- Page 1304 ---
1197 days. By the second impugned order, the review
petition was also dismissed.
4. Having regard to the fact that there was no
satisfactory explanation for the delay, we do not
find it to be a fit case for interference.
5. The  special  leave  petition  is,  accordingly,
dismissed on both delay and merits.
6. Pending  application(s),  if  any,  shall  stand
disposed of. 
(CHETAN ARORA)                                  (DIVYA BABBAR)

                   Mr. P. S. Sudheer, AOR                         
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
Learned  counsel  appearing  for  the  parties
jointly submitted that the parties have entered into
out of Court settlement and prays that the matter be
referred to mediation.
Learned  counsel  appearing  for  the  parties
further submits that matter be referred to the Kerala
State Mediation and Conciliation Centre (for short
“KSMCC”) so that necessary settlement deed can be
entered  into  and  produced  before  the  Court  and
ultimately the matter can be settled.
1

--- Page 1324 ---
In view of the aforesaid joint request made by
learned counsel for the parties, the matter is referred
to the KSMCC.
The parties shall appear either physically or
virtually  before  the  Coordinator  of  the  Mediation
Centre on 05th August at 11:00 a.m.
In case personal appearance of the parties is
required, the parties will abide by the directions of
the learned Mediator.
The learned Mediator shall submit a report to
this Court within a period of 12 weeks from receipt of
this order.
The Registry shall ensure that a copy of this
order along with the relevant documents is forwarded to
the KSMCC at the earliest.
List the matter after the mediation report is
received.
(PRIYANKA MALIK)                                (JAYANT RAJ KATYAL)
COURT MASTER (SH)                                  BRANCH OFFICER
2

ITEM NO.1701               COURT NO.12               SECTION II-E
               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
SPECIAL LEAVE PETITION (CRIMINAL) Diary No(s). 34241/2026
ABHISHEK RATNAM                                    Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
THE STATE OF MADHYA PRADESH                        Respondent(s)
FOR ADMISSION 
IA No. 192839/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM CUSTODY CERTIFICATE
IA No. 192837/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T.
 
Date : 17-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE VIPUL M. PANCHOLI
                           [IN CHAMBER]
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. F. I. Choudhury, AOR
                   Mr. Aditya Sharma, Adv.
                   Mr. David Choudhury, Adv.                   
                   
For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
IA No. 192839/2026
The present application has been filed with the
prayer that the petitioner be exempted from filing the
custody certificate. 
Learned counsel for the petitioner has referred to
the averments made in this application and thereafter
referred to the impuged order dated 28.04.2026 passed by
the concerned High Court, whereby the High Court has
though suspended the sentence of the petitioner, imposed
the condition to deposit a sum of Rs.87,00,000/- which was
subsequently reduced to 50% of the original fine amount.
1

--- Page 1326 ---
It is submitted by the learned counsel for the petitioner
that the petitioner is not in a position to pay the said
amount  and,  therefore,  the  petitioner  has  filed  this
special leave petition before this Court.
Learned counsel has also referred to para 3 of the
impugned order wherein it has been specifically stated
that the custody report was produced before the concerned
High Court stating mentioned that the petitioner remained
in custody from 15.08.2017 to 27.12.2019 during the course
of the trial and has been in continuous custody since the
date of judgment of conviction passed by the concerned
Trial Court i.e. 13.12.2024. In fact, the actual sentence
undergone by the petitioner was 3 years 11 months and 16
days, including remission. 
Learned counsel, therefore, urged that looking into
the facts and circumstances of the present case when there
is a specific reference in the impugned order pointing out
the  period  during  which  the  petitioner  remained  in
custody, the petitioner be exempted from filing custody
certificate as prayed for in this application.
Considering the submissions canvassed by the learned
counsel  appearing  for  the  petitioner  and  having  gone
through  the  material  placed  on  record,  the  present
application is allowed.
(PRIYANKA MALIK)                                (JAYANT RAJ KATYAL)
COURT MASTER (SH)                                 BRANCH OFFICER
2

--- Page 1327 ---

from participating in any tender process till then.

RUBY & ANR.                                        Respondent(s)
IA No. 191036/2026 - CONDONATION OF DELAY IN FILING, IA No.
191034/2026 - PERMISSION TO FILE PETITION (SLP/TP/WP/..)
 
Date : 15-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE K.V. VISWANATHAN
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE ARUN PALLI
For Petitioner(s)  Ms. Rashmi Nandakumar, AOR
                   Ms. Yashmita Pandey, Adv.                   
                   
For Respondent(s) Mr. Brijender Chahar, ASG
Mr. Abhinav Singh, Adv.
Mr. Karan Chahar, Adv.
Mr. Kartik K.Sharma, Adv.
Mr. S.N.Terdal, AOR
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Permission to file Special Leave Petition is granted.
2 Delay condoned.
3. Heard Mr. Rashmi Nandakumar, learned counsel for the
petitioner  and  Mr.  Brijender  Chahar,  learned  Additional
Solicitor General appearing for the State.
4. Having perused the impugned order, we are not inclined to
interfere with the same.
5. At this stage, learned counsel for the petitioner submits
that if the respondent no.1 is enlarged on bail, claim of
parity will arise.

--- Page 1386 ---
2
6. That apprehension is not justified for the reason that
the respondent no.1 has been enlarged on bail considering the
fact that she is a lady and more particularly, considering her
medical condition. This  factum is not even disputed by the
learned counsel for the petitioner. Further, respondent no.1
has already undergone about one year and six months as an
under  trial.  In  view  of  all  these  peculiarities,  the
respondent no.1’s case stands on a separate footing.
7. With the above observations, the Special Leave Petition
is dismissed.
8. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of.
(NIRMALA NEGI)                                  (MANOJ KUMAR)
ASTT. REGISTRAR-cum-PS                        COURT MASTER (NSH)

by ways of email.

ITEM NO.5               COURT NO.8               SECTION II
               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Petition for Special Leave to Appeal (Crl.) No. 9743/2026
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated  07-11-2025
in CRA No. 3735/2025 passed by the High Court of Judicature at 
Allahabad]
MUSARRAT ALI                                       Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
STATE OF UTTAR PRADESH & ANR.                      Respondent(s)
(IA No. 190454/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T.)
 
Date : 20-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE SANJAY KUMAR
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE SANJEEV SACHDEVA
For Petitioner(s) Mr. Akhand Pratap Singh Chauhan, Adv.
                   Mr. Tungesh, AOR
                   Mr. Sachin Sharma, Adv.
                   Dr. Shipra Tripathi, Adv.
                   Mr. Pravesh Sharma, Adv.
                                      
For Respondent(s) Mr. Ankit Goel, AOR
                   Mr. Mayank Tiwari, Adv.
                   Ms. Dipika Kalia, Adv.
                                      
      UPON hearing the counsel, the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
 From the counter affidavit filed by the State of Uttar
Pradesh,  we  find  that  four  criminal  cases/First  Information
Reports(FIRs) were registered against the petitioner, Musarrat Ali,
prior to the filing of this special leave petition. Two of the said
FIRs find mention in the special leave petition paper book and it
is stated that one FIR of the year 2026 was registered only after
the imprisonment of the petitioner and he was unaware of said fact.
1

--- Page 1408 ---
However, the petitioner admittedly failed to disclose one case
registered against him as long back as in the year 2020.
In the light of the law laid down by this Court in “Munnesh
vs. State of Uttar Pradesh” 1, non-disclosure of a past antecedent
by the petitioner is fatal to his case. 
The special leave petition is, accordingly, dismissed on this
short ground. 
Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of.
 (BABITA PANDEY)                                (PREETI SAXENA)
    AR-cum-PS                           COURT MASTER (NSH)
1 2025 SCC OnLine SC 1319
2

--- Page 1409 ---
1

               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Petition(s) for Special Leave to Appeal (Crl.)  No(s).  11748/2025
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated  07-07-2025
in CRM-M No. 30453/2025 passed by the High Court of Punjab &
Haryana at Chandigarh]
SHEIKH ABDUL MAJEED                                Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
STATE OF PUNJAB & ANR.                             Respondent(s)
[MEDIATION REPORT RECEIVED] 
IA No. 295819/2025 - APPLICATION FOR PERMISSION
IA No. 188256/2025 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T.
IA No. 295821/2025 - PERMISSION TO FILE ADDITIONAL 
DOCUMENTS/FACTS/ANNEXURES
 
Date : 20-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE ARAVIND KUMAR
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE VIPUL M. PANCHOLI
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. Amjid Maqbool, Adv.
                   Ms. Pallavi Pratap, AOR                   
                   
For Respondent(s) :Ms. Abha Sharma, AOR
                   Mr. Anupam Maurya, Adv.
                   Mr. Praneet Das, Adv.
                   Mr. Rahul Singh, Adv.
                   
                   Mr. Nischal Kumar Neeraj, AOR
                   
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Mr. Nischal Kumar Neeraj, learned AOR, appearing for the
complainant submits that the complainant has received the entire
amount due and admits the contents of the affidavit filed by the

          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R

that the parties wish to explore the possibility of settlement  
through mediation and request that they may be referred to the  
Supreme Court Mediation Centre.
3. In view of the statements as made, the parties are referred for  
mediation.  The  parties  are  directed  to  appear  before  the  
Coordinator, Supreme Court Mediation Centre virtually on the first  
hearing on 31.07.2026 at 11:00 A.M. and in case the learned  
Mediator requires the physical presence of the parties, the parties  
may appear physically as may be decided by the learned Mediator.
4. Re-list immediately upon receipt of a report from the learned  
Mediator.

directed  to  take  steps  for  listing  the  matter  before  the
appropriate Bench at the earliest.

stands disposed of.

CORAM : HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE SANJAY KUMAR
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE SANJEEV SACHDEVA
For Petitioner(s) Mr. K. Subramanian, Sr. Adv.
                   Mr. E. C. Agrawala, AOR
                   Ms. Kamakshi Sehgal, Adv.
                   Mr. Rajesh Kumar, Adv.
                   Mr. Pankaj Agarwal, Adv.
                   Mr. Siddhant Sahay, Adv.                       
For Respondent(s)  
      UPON hearing the counsel, the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
 In the light of the law laid down in “ M/s. Universal Sompo
General Insurance Co. Ltd. vs. Suresh Chand Jain & Anr. 1”, the
petitioner  has  an  effective  alternative  remedy  before  the
jurisdictional High Court.
The special leave petition is dismissed, leaving it open to
the petitioner to avail such remedy in accordance with law.
Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of.
 (BABITA PANDEY)                                (PREETI SAXENA)
